## Message Group #228895

> Replying to NCNF: sure thing. thanks

mehowz: I’ll tell you this: scan the website, but more importantly scan all the articles you got linked above, and go through all the Zenon medium posts. It’ll be hard, but treat it like homework. Print them all and take a few days to go over it all. If there’s anything you don’t get (which there will be), put a question mark next to it. Compile a list. Then ask other questions ie can I make some APYs by holding ZNN? Why would the asset accrue value over time? Questions that are relevant to investors looking to build portfolios.

mehowz: Then come back and be relentless on questions. People will help, but you may not get all your answers instantly. Be patient.

mehowz: Slowly pieces will come together in your mind and a vision will form

## Message Group #247017
Date: 2022-07-04T01:31:17Z

mehowz: https://twitter.com/ZenonOrg/status/1543768545619738626?s=20

---

## Message Group #247096
Date: 2022-07-04T17:16:12Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/247096

> Replying to Ex-Fang Tech Lead Final Boss: If I may provide one bit of feedback to everyone. When I first found thorchain I discovered thorchain.org first. It was by far many times better than the thorchain.com website. I thought .org was the 

mehowz: Thanks for the feedback, I designed the .org 🙂 I think the only thing that’ll be official about the zenon.org brand will be that many of its sub-domain tools will be operated by the contributing community (ie docs site, newsletter issues etc). IMO it’s important for an entity to take leadership to aggregate the whole as one — and I do plan to try to outshine the zenon.network website with my work on the zenon.org (and related tools). 

We had a choice to centralize the entire zenon.org brand, and chose against it i.e. many of the stuff we ask for collabs in the forums, could’ve all be done centrally by us — we have the funds to hire if we wanted-to. Instead we preferred to let community develop their own A-Z proposals using the tools and brand name (subdomains) to make the ecosystem more unified.

We may also lead to collabs with other pillar operators. I’m inspired by the fact that some pillar operators are willing to contribute to a pillar-owned brand name, for the well-being of network growth. I will find a balance of featuring contributing entities on the upcoming org, including pillars that do compete with ZenonORG pillars. It’s tricky, but I’m sure it can be done.

---

## Message Group #247099
Date: 2022-07-04T17:44:45Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/247099

> Replying to Ex-Fang Tech Lead Final Boss: I think zenon.org is the perfect place to grow the community.  Who knows how Pillar "competition" will unfold in the future.  At this point we need to expand our footprint and .org is a great place to

mehowz: The .org helped a lot to direct traffic, but really the trick was to be loud in the outbound. I don’t know if you remember TC in 2019-2020 — they were crazy loud, and gained a huge community. And hence why I’m pushing for these spaces of organized content, whether it be via a well built docs site, or curated newsletter issues from community experts on various topics. The .org brand would do its thing to be loud alongside community and distribute the content in relevant outbound feeds. With an attribute.me integration, whoever shares any zenon.org landing page will also be attributed any conversions that may have occured on zenon.org — or Zenon ecosystem sites i.e. imagine someone builds a DEX and integrates in the ecosystem attribute systems, if someone shared a link to any zenon.org page, and incoming traffic ends up performing swaps on the DEX, that user who originally shared that link, would be attributed the swap conversions. All in a public dashboard on Attribute.me. Just so many opportunities with working together as one ecosystem rather than duplicate tools trying to solve the same problem. ZenonOrg will be nothing more than a tool to get traffic from point A to point Z. Looking forward to being loud once we roll out the .org site!

mehowz: The Build & Support channel is the best space for you to meet other devs: https://t.me/zenon_builders

---

## Message Group #279376
Date: 2023-04-11T15:58:34Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/279376

> Replying to Mr. HeiZNNberg: Can I use Attribute to track new people who are joining ZNN through HZNN Playground tg channel?

mehowz: It cannot. It tracks traffic on Zenon.Org at the moment — though Attribute does support cross-domain tracking, so eventually if the eosystem develops its own websites, they can all use Attribute and share user sessions between all ecosystem sites. Data would become available on Attribute at that point, if the site integrates event triggers like Zenon.Org did.

## Message Group #279388
Date: 2023-04-11T18:12:58Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/279388

> Replying to blue: Each time someone clicks my link, I get $ZNN?

mehowz: No you don’t get $ZNN at the moment. Eventually maybe a payment layer can be architected on top using TSS. Something @su_mamu and I have chatted about briefly yesterday. But before we get there we need to complete syrius web/walletconnect funnels, prove the full cycle of participation acquisition, and rearchitect Attribute. The current version of Attribute is designed to be a proof of concept to learn from. At the moment, the goal for the community is to learn how to market Zenon on their own using organic methods i.e. engage in social (listen for relevant conversations and add value with their links).

---

## Message Group #280794
Date: 2023-04-15T01:42:39Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/280794

mehowz: https://twitter.com/mehowbrainz/status/1647051636164968449?s=20

---

## Message Group #281404
Date: 2023-04-15T21:26:34Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/281404

> Replying to 5790160614: OK, I'm going to float an idea and please thumbs up or thumbs down the post so we can gauge community sentiment.

AZ Proposal: Fund You Tube Ad campaign.

Proposal is for max amount 5000znn 50000qsr
T

mehowz: A few things missing in this strategy:

1) Proof of concept to validate whether the content/ad converts engagment into the next stage of the funnel (the landing page).

2) Once the ad ends where is the user driven to? A landing page? which one? What’s the action?

3) What other metrics will be measured past ad views to deem that the campaign lead to new value to the network? Page views of the landing page? Clicks on whichever Attribute event? Given the Attribute funnels for syrius aren’t completed yet, we actually can’t know whether someone delegated, staked, installed the wallet just yet.

4) To say that it will bring 1-5K new users is a massive assumption, nor will we ever be able to prove that they’re participants (given the lack of integrations atm). Maybe landing page viewers, maybe? But users, not sure about that at all. I’d think a fraction of 1-5K if we’re lucky.

5) What’s the participant you’re focusing on in the campaign? New wallet installs? New pillars? New devs? We can’t target them all in 1 campaign. Such ideas are too general and IMO we’re past that stage of assumptions, we need proposals which at the bare minimum can answer every point the guidelines suggest: https://forum.zenon.org/t/performance-marketing-proposal-guidelines/918?u=mehowbrainz

6) Is YouTube friendly to blockchain/crypto ads? Might want to re-evaluate that assumption. Every major network including Twitter are difficult on crypto ads….

7) Ad spend requires retargeting to drive back value from the ad spent, which hasn’t been integrated yet into zenon.org funnels nor Attribute. Without it, once you spend and that user clicks, you have no way to drive him back to your content/channels.

I’m all for the excitement to drive awareness to the network, but IMO we’re not ready yet for ad spend. You can easily burn funds and have no ability to measure success if other tooling isn’t implemented/ready yet — and so far the zenon.org funnels don’t have the syrius / walletconnect integrations (nor are they developed yet), so for that reason I would argue to hold off from even considering ad spend at this stage. Anyone who does will get grilled on the lack of ability to justify success.

A better strategy would be for the community to roll up their sleeves, go on Tweet Deck and other social networks/tools — and begin to listen-for conversations that are fitting for Zenon, and paste their Attribute links in mix with contextual content that adds value to generate that traffic and engagement. You’d be surprised how many impressions tweets can gets if they’re positioned in the right conversations / say the right things. 

Attribute campaigning tutorial: https://twitter.com/AttributeLink/status/1644361381074837505

Many Attribute campaigners are driving some traffie, we can crank this up by 100x easily just with focus and dedicated by the community to become strategic marketers.

Ads are good for top of funnel awareness. But we just don’t have all the tools in place to measure full cycle yet…

---

## Message Group #281410
Date: 2023-04-15T21:46:24Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/281410

> Replying to 5790160614: Thanks for the detailed response.

I think your marketing sounds very detailed and results and metrics driven.

But, and please don't take this is a slight or criticism, it has yet to yield any tangib

mehowz: As much as I truly appreciate the drive to bring in new users, I think the proposal lacks experience and has too many assumptions. If we have to spend for the sake of spending without everything else ready, I’d say that we have a better shot at paying a influencers for content (but I would also vote against those too). Crypto in general is referral / trust based, and it’s best to really evaluate which channels / audiences / strategies are being used. Also getting articles up on some major crypto news sites via a PR push would be another way to drive visibility. Or using native ads (as they’re embedded on sites to look like part of the content of the site, which adds that trust layer). I think the proposal misses that mark on how crypto works, and visibility/impressions isn’t the only variable in play.

mehowz: The problem with the deck is that it hasn’t been used by anyone strategically. You can’t make a piece of content and think that it’ll get visibility just cause it exists. It was a tool designed for business development / email / referral strategies. No one built a campaign around it, it’s no wonder it hasn’t paid itself off yet! We have so many tools at our disposal, and no one is using them. The main Zenon video on Twitter is another. If we all just rolled up our sleeves and starting being out there, we’d 100x our impressions.

mehowz: The community is stuck in this Telegram channel. We need to leave this safe nest and begin talk in the outbound. I’ve been saying this for ages.

---

mehowz: The difference is that on Twitter you need to find that audience and join the conversation that isn’t inbound coming your way (like it is here, which someone joins and says “Tell me about Zenon” and then the shills begin).

## Message Group #281561
Date: 2023-04-15T22:55:16Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/281561

> Replying to 5790160614: 100%

The proposal is not a marketing campaign.

It is to raise funds to pay for YouTube ads. Thats all.

If your fully fledged marketing campaign works well then this will be a value add on top of th

mehowz: Every request from AZ should have a clear explanation of the value it will drive to the network. In your case it’s that a certain percent of viewers will watch 6 seconds, another will watch past 15 seconds, another will watch past 60 seconds, and another will watch 100% (you won’t know your % upfront either). Great that’s all the value you can prove that was driven. Now you’ll ask the network if this value is worth the $10K spend, at the current ZNN valuation. The bigger question is: will this $10K spend influence an increase in the valuation of ZNN today, tomorrow, in 1 month, 6 months?

## Message Group #316807
Date: 2023-10-10T14:11:36Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/316807

> Replying to Shazz: Pls dont tweet stuff like this. Tweets should be managed by dedicated marketers. Anyone else has no business approving tweets.

mehowz: I'm all for Zenon_Network sharing stuff from the community, but I think we need to agree on standards before blasting them on the Zenon_Network channel. Now that we know about this channel, maybe the person behind it could get some feedback to tweak the style so it vibes better with the community and still keeps that professional look we're trying to portray to Zenon. To me, it seems a bit rushed to just throw something out there using some AI voiceover tech. With a little more digging, the creator could have found templates, tools, or ideas to make it look a bit more professional. I don’t think we need a mascot or avatar for the Zenon brand.

When I was putting together Zenon.Org, I didn’t want to just throw in any random images and mess up the look. I was short on time and good art that fits our brand can cost quite a bit to produce. And I couldn't rely on AI yet,  I really tried even for the most abstract ideas. So, I went for a clean, simple look instead of stuffing in some so-so graphics that could throw off our brand vibe. Visuals will come later.

My suggestion to creators is: less is more, always.

mehowz: Use attribute affiliate links instead of using the direct zenon.org URL. You can pick any zenon.org page in the link builder list, and in the very near future we will have new lightweight funnels to help convert even more.

mehowz: I'll show you how

mehowz: https://x.com/ZenonOrg/status/1726750517097906667?s=20

mehowz: If people end up swapping wZNN to ZNN after they visit zenon.org from your link, you will earn the 2% referral

mehowz: In an upcoming update, the Attribute dashboard will be able to also show that your Attribute campaign affiliate links are responsible for a wZNN > ZNN swap, and how much.

## Message Group #329402
Date: 2023-11-21T08:22:55Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/329402

mehowz: No you have to use the link builder on attribute.zenon.org to build yourself a new link

mehowz: see the guide tweet I posted above

mehowz: The newly built link will go to the same page, but the Attribute one will have your zenon address + campaign name set as a UTM parameter for tracking.

mehowz: And with that zenon address parameter, the website will detect your address and automatically save you as the referrer for the user who clicked on the link.

mehowz: If that user at some point later swaps from wZNN to ZNN using the bridge, you earn the referral

mehowz: You're basically calling first dibs on that user who landed on zenon.org via your attribute link

mehowz: You can make yourself as many Attribute campaign links as you want. You can go granular and make one for each new tweet you write.

mehowz: Fill the campaign field with a unique name/id so that you can personally track which one of your tweets is doing what on the website

mehowz: Attribute.zenon.org lets you see what people are doing once they click on one of your affiliate links. Are they visiting the bridge? Are they clicking on the whitepaper? Are they downloading syrius?

mehowz: Attributes measures all traffic touchpoints, and attributes them to the marketers who brought them there.

mehowz: While giving you dibs on the referral if the traffic swaps wZNN > ZNN via the bridge.

---

## Message Group #343727
Date: 2024-01-07T16:22:47Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/343727

> Replying to mehowz | ZenonOrg: How is that possible, I have 2FA and all enabled. Checking.

mehowz: Bizarre, my Discord account was hacked but it's not understood how:

- They didn't change my password because I was able to change it back.
- Had 2FA enabled.
- Had SMS disabled.
- Email address unaffected.
- My phone number was not associated to my account.
- They changed my name to a curse word, what's the point?

---

## Message Group #343737
Date: 2024-01-07T17:07:22Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/343737

mehowz: Community Notice: Important Security Incident and Apologies

I am writing to you today to inform you of a recent security incident involving my Discord account. A user, posing as a journalist named Abdul, contacted me on Discord, expressing interest in press inquiries related to our community (posing as ournetwork.xyz -- a legitimate publication it seems). He directed me to join the OurNetwork Discord server to make a ticket for our discussion. The served seemed legitimate, posing as a workspace for the brand and private space for its subscribers. Upon joining, I was prompted to verify my account using an external link. The verification process appeared authentic, and without suspecting foul play, I proceeded. But the verification was in fact a "self-XSS (Cross Site Scripting)" attack. It's designed to deceive users into executing harmful JavaScript code in their web browser, particularly in the context of a web application like Discord.

In my naivety, I assumed this external verification was a special requirement for that particular server. I've since learned that Discord does not use external sites for server verification, and realizing this was a sophisticated hacking attempt, I feel responsible for not being more vigilant.

Thankfully, my password wasn't changed because I had 2FA enabled. My email address and phone number (which wasn't associated with my account) remained unaffected. However, the breach could have had far more severe consequences. I want to emphasize that all my accounts are highly secured. This incident was not a result of inadequate security setup but rather a sophisticated type of attack that exploited a different kind of vulnerability - my trust in the legitimacy of external verification processes.

I sincerely apologize for any concerns this might have caused. This incident was a stark reminder of the sophistication of online threats and the need for constant vigilance.

Please be aware that Discord does not use external sites for server verification and always be skeptical of such requests, especially external verifications. Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for an added layer of security.

I take full responsibility for this oversight and promise to be more cautious in the future. Let's all use this incident as a learning opportunity to strengthen our community's security and awareness.

Stay safe and secure,
mehowz

---

## Message Group #343950

> Replying to Stark: Interesting. Also illustrates why many/most got zero affiliate rewards for known successful shills. No rewards if they don't bridge.

## Message Group #343958
Date: 2024-01-08T18:16:53Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/343958

mehowz: We call those preframe bridges in marketing: articles, news, blog posts, videos, emails, presell pages... designed in a funnel form to keep the user engaged. Next they get qualified via some squeeze page (can be an incentive that they'll get the 1% bonus on bridging). The qualification is asking them for some basic info, their email or TG handle so we have something to work with (retargetting) -- and also have retargeting ad networks to show them more Zenon in the outbound as they browse the web (based on what the systems knows they read). The whole idea of funnels is to shortcut the process to convert someone into becoming a participant into the network, without sounding salesy or scammy. Make it natural. Content creators are good for the preframe bridge creation, but they have to know what to lead their traffic into. I will demonstrate examples of this shortly as I roll out first funnels for core needs i.e. bridging, buying wznn etc.

---

## Message Group #343962
Date: 2024-01-08T18:20:13Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/343962

> Replying to CZ: Also onboarded a few friends , but if they did the research by themselves they would not invest in the project because its simply too complex for them. Thorchain for example also had twitter accounts 

mehowz: THORChain held a lot of hands with community enabled spaces like thornoobs and lp university -- but I think we can do better and make it so simple where it's automated to an extent. Community can kick in for support where needed. With metrics we'll figure out where the weak stages of the funnels are.

---

## Message Group #344020
Date: 2024-01-08T20:46:33Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/344020

mehowz: For those on desktop atm, here's an interactive prototype for the "Download the Full Deck" funnel:
https://zenonorg815913.invisionapp.com/console/share/ZE3CSHN8F9A/987756331

I use a lead magnet for the first step to capture their email address. Ideally I'd also like their Telegram handle as well, but  I don't want to overwhelm them so I prioritize what's most important for me: getting their email so that I could automatically nurture them over the long run. I will be testing methods in some future to nurture automatically via Telegram as well.

Therefore I keep the "give me your telegram handle" on the second step once the user has entered their email address. I use an OTO page (one-time-offer). It's designed to upsell and get them to give their TG handle in exchange for the 1% bonus activation. That bonus would get activated regardless because all ZenonOrg landing pages automatically enable it for users as soon as they land, but they don't know that. So that's an opportunity for me to get something extra. I do give them the option to skip at this upsell step however.

At the third step, I initiative the download of the Full Deck. I then try to upsell them again, this time bridging them into the "Buy wZNN on Uniswap" step. Their bonus is activated already, so they may feel inclined to go through with the purchase after they've read the deck? We will see.

The 4th step would be to lead them into the bridge once we qualify that they've purchased wZNN at the 3rd step.

At every step I know what users did, or didn't do. Since I have their email address I can begin to nurture email campaigns to help them resume their onboarding into the network.

These funnels will be brandable to ecosystem brands i.e. zenon.info, build_ etc. They will also be added to the Attribute library so anyone can promote any flavour.

I'm developing a dozen of these, for all types of downloads i.e. syrius, nodes, guides, buying wZNN and more.

Once these core funnels are done, I will then transition my efforts on preframe bridges as mentioned here: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/343958 -- will be designed to make newcomers aware and try to shortcut their onboarding into wZNN > ZNN swaps, mixed with targetted outbound campaigns to present Zenon to fresh eyes.

---

## Message Group #365551
Date: 2024-04-26T19:26:43Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/365551

mehowz: Timeline of events so far this week:

- They try to swoop control over another channel: website.

- They disagree with the notion that OG founded channels are network-owned.

- They disagree witha democratic approach to channel ownership/admins/maintainers via this brainstorming: https://forum.zenon.org/t/network-governance-brainstorming/1878

- They lie and fabricate facts. Some are caught red handed. See above.

## Message Group #372294
Date: 2024-07-17T13:30:45Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/372294

> Replying to romeo: As a new Twitter handle, what's the plan to get more eyes on the tweets? Or is it more of a complementary aspect to the main campaign

mehowz: To engage with audiences relevant to the Zenon demand/supply matrix. I’m inspired by a concept in sociology named complex contagions, as described in the manifesto on the website:

“In our interconnected world, the spread of revolutionary ideas is akin to momentum building. Unlike simple transmissions, which spread quickly from one individual to another, a shift in momentum requires initiative and reinforcement. It is not enough for one person to advocate for change; we are building a collective chorus of voices, a critical mass of influence, to inspire true adoption.”

"Revolutions are often born on the outskirts of social networks, where smaller, tightly-knit groups of individuals share strong connections and mutual reinforcement. It is within these peripheral areas that the initial sparks of change ignite, eventually spreading to the broader network. This revolution must begin with us, the dedicated few who understand the necessity of a paradigm shift in the blockchain industry.”

mehowz: https://youtu.be/Y-1qLR2w60M

mehowz: It’s in French, use some translate tool

mehowz: https://x.com/mehowbrainz/status/1813589205005525487

mehowz: https://x.com/SatoshisL1/status/1813614377058312678

## Message Group #372536
Date: 2024-07-23T14:27:24Z
Telegram: https://t.me/zenonnetwork/372536

mehowz: Is there a forum thread to discuss?

mehowz: My 2c re: https://www.greenpill.pro/grass-roots-marketing-az

Imma post a bunch of pricings from media kits soon — you’ll see why content alone without distribution cannot be worth 5K + 50K QSR. 

Quick example: A newsletter campaign with one of the major sources can cost around $2800 ($10 CPM), that’ll be sent out to 280K readers, 35% will open so 98K. So you know you’ll get 98K reads for that cost. Then 0.7% will open a link in the newsletter article (this drives down into whichever site — aka funnel but these people don’t get that a funnel is nothing more than a symbolic term to represent a path down a journey of discovery until participation is proven — even JP from THORChain who was against marketing terms started to use funnel in his latest meme strat lingo, just saying Deez you really gotta up your game here, poking doesn’t work). So at 0.7% clicks, you may get 686 clicks.

Value driven from the $2.8K = 98,000 reads of your Zenon content and 686 clicks down any link to discover any site (funnel).

Regardless of the quality of his content. As a whole, did they generate 98,000 reads to new potential participants? Or was his content shared around Zenon bag holders?

This is a data-driven game whether you like it or not.

I have no issues with paying for content like I always said, but it should be coupled with a decent distribution strategy. My vote would be to repurpose his content into sponsored content on a variety of sources (I compiled a bunch), and pay Zyler a fraction of the fee he asked + spend the funds on distribution like mentioned above. This’ll give him a pathway to participate with new content again that’s actually being distributed properly to audiences. I’m open to collaborating with Zyler 2.0 if he wishes-to. My votes would forego the conversion aspects but it would be very helpful to put in a new bridge redirect link (soon testing with sumamu) somewhere there so you can get soooome metrics if new participation occurs eventually (I can help with setting UTM parameters in it). I can’t expect too much.

P.S. I didn’t vote to be part of any Pillar Alliance (was on vacation). I’ll always argue what’s right or wrong for the network’s AZ.

---
